description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped stone with a predominantly off-white surface, accented by patches of brown and tan discoloration. The stone's surface is uneven, featuring a mix of rounded and jagged protrusions, as well as several shallow crevices and indentations. Its texture appears rough and natural, with subtle variations in color suggesting mineral deposits or weathering. The stone has a somewhat lumpy, asymmetrical form, with one side appearing broader and flatter while the opposite side tapers to a more angular edge. There are no visible signs of artificial modification, carvings, or tool marks. The overall appearance suggests a naturally occurring stone, possibly used as an obstacle or barrier in an urban park, sidewalk, or landscaped area.
description: An irregular, off-white stone with brown patches and a rough, weathered surface. Its lumpy, asymmetrical form features both rounded and jagged edges.
description_view_0: The stone appears broad and rounded, with visible brown discoloration and a prominent indentation on the right side.
description_view_1: This side is more angular and elongated, showing a pronounced ridge and less surface discoloration.
description_view_2: The stone looks bulkier, with deeper crevices and darker brown patches concentrated near the bottom.
description_view_3: A smoother, more tapered side with minimal surface markings and a subtle orange-brown tint near the top.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or obstacle on the ground
attribute_pattern: Irregular mottling with subtle brown and tan patches, no regular veining or artificial carvings.
attribute_weathering: Surface is rough and uneven, with visible discoloration suggesting moderate natural weathering.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Typically contrasts with paved surfaces but may blend with natural landscaping elements such as soil, mulch, or other stones.
